fellows.name: Emefa Awo Adawudu
fellows.focus: Type 2 diabetes, SDOH and self-management in Black and African Americans
fellows.bio: A PhD candidate at the University of Massachusetts Amherst, Adawudu's research focuses on the intersection of psychosocial aspects of type 2 diabetes, social determinants of health, and self-management practices among Black and African Americans.

fellows.name: David Chinyeaka Agor
fellows.institution: University of Pennsylvania School of Nursing
fellows.focus: Mental health access for marginalized populations
fellows.bio: Agor is a PhD student at the University of Pennsylvania School of Nursing. A triple-certified psychiatric mental health nurse practitioner, HIV primary care provider, and nutritional psychiatry specialist, he integrates clinical expertise with advanced analytics to address SDOH and strengthen health care access to improve outcomes for marginalized populations.

fellows.name: Natalia Babenko
fellows.focus: Well-being of older adults
fellows.bio: A doctoral student in aging studies at the University of South Florida, Babenko is dedicated to aging-related initiatives, serving as a volunteer with the Alzheimer's Association and leading public health education efforts. Her research focuses on improving the well-being of older adults.

fellows.name: Rajib Kumar Biswas
fellows.focus: Dementia caregiving, behavioral intervention development, SDOH, and injury prevention
fellows.bio: Biswas is a PhD student at the Nell Hodgson Woodruff School of Nursing at Emory University. His research centers on dementia caregiving and the development of behavioral interventions, with an emphasis on injury prevention among people living with Alzheimer's disease and related dementias. Drawing on more than a decade of clinical and research experience, he examines how social determinants of health shape outcomes for vulnerable populations.

fellows.name: Yanjun Dong
fellows.focus: Health care disparities among older adults
fellows.bio: Dong is a PhD candidate at the University at Albany, SUNY. Her research examines health care disparities among older adults, particularly how socioeconomic, environmental, and policy factors affect access to care.

fellows.name: Aisha Ellis
fellows.focus: SDOH and health care use among formerly incarcerated adults
fellows.bio: A PhD student at the Johns Hopkins School of Nursing, Ellis investigates the relationship between SDOH, health care utilization, and outcomes in formerly incarcerated adults with mental illness.

fellows.name: Taleah Frazier
fellows.focus: Cardiometabolic risk in reproductive-aged Black women
fellows.bio: A PhD student at the University of North Carolina, Frazier examines multilevel factors driving stress-related cardiometabolic risk in reproductive-aged Black women, aiming to shape maternal health policy and interventions.

fellows.name: John A. Fuller
fellows.focus: Intersectional stigma among Black LGBTQ+ people with HIV and cancer
fellows.bio: Fuller is a PhD student at the Emory School of Nursing whose research explores how intersectional stigma, social support, and psychosocial determinants influence health outcomes among Black LGBTQ+ people living with HIV and cancer.

fellows.name: Laura Mata Lopez
fellows.focus: Mental health and substance use care for Latino(a) immigrants
fellows.bio: Lopez is a PhD candidate at the Johns Hopkins University School of Nursing and psychiatric nurse practitioner. She focuses on accessible mental health and substance use care for Latino(a) immigrants in under-resourced communities.

fellows.name: Andrew Lu
fellows.focus: Coding bias in ECG algorithms and SDOH
fellows.bio: Lu is a PhD student in Emory's nursing-AI track who specializes in nursing informatics and data science, investigating coding bias in ECG algorithms based on SDOH.

fellows.name: Maria Jose Baeza Robba
fellows.focus: Gender-based violence and help-seeking behaviors
fellows.bio: A postdoctoral fellow at the University of Michigan, Baeza Robba researches gender-based violence and help-seeking behaviors among women from various cultural backgrounds.

fellows.name: Dalton Scott
fellows.focus: Intergenerational effects of adverse childhood experiences
fellows.bio: Scott is a PhD candidate at the University of Miami. He applies an intersectional approach to the ecodevelopmental model to prevent and mitigate the intergenerational effects of adverse childhood experiences, particularly within immigrant communities.

fellows.name: Curisa Tucker
fellows.institution: University of South Carolina College of Nursing
fellows.focus: SDOH and maternal health
fellows.bio: An assistant professor at the University of South Carolina College of Nursing and a former postdoctoral fellow at Stanford University, Tucker's work addresses social determinants of health that impact maternal health, including severe maternal morbidity and postpartum readmissions.

fellows.name: Caitlin Webster
fellows.focus: SDOH, the microbiome and pediatric oncology
fellows.bio: A PhD candidate at the Emory School of Nursing, Webster studies the relationship between SDOH, the microbiome, and treatment-related toxicities in pediatric oncology patients.

fellows.name: Abigail Julier Beliveau
fellows.institution: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
fellows.focus: Stigma and discrimination as barriers to harm reduction
fellows.bio: Beliveau studies non-biological influences such as stigma and discrimination within healthcare and institutional settings that limit community members' engagement in harm reduction and life-saving techniques.

fellows.name: Ashley Bryant
fellows.focus: Sleep, stress and glycemic control in Black women with diabetes
fellows.bio: Bryant frames sleep and physiological recovery as safety priorities, examining how social, cultural, environmental, and policy factors influence sleep, chronic stress, and glycemic control among African American women with diabetes in the Southern United States.

fellows.name: Jasmine Dixon
fellows.focus: Biopsychosocial factors in cognitive aging and Alzheimer's
fellows.bio: Dixon's research focuses on how multi-level biopsychosocial factors — vascular disease, depression, stress, social engagement, and social determinants of health — interact to influence healthy cognitive aging and the risk of Alzheimer's disease in underserved populations.

fellows.name: Sonja Emerson
fellows.focus: Breastfeeding and housing insecurity
fellows.bio: Emerson studies breastfeeding and infant feeding as health behaviors shaped by social, environmental, and policy contexts, with particular interest in housing insecurity as a structural determinant of breastfeeding.

fellows.name: Charles Nukunu Gagbe
fellows.focus: PrEP uptake among Black sexual minority men
fellows.bio: Gagbe's work sits at the intersection of HIV prevention, health equity, and SDOH, focusing on how stigma and medical mistrust shape disparities in PrEP uptake and adherence among Black sexual minority men in Southern Florida.

fellows.name: Tammy Hall
fellows.institution: University of North Carolina at Greensboro
fellows.focus: Rural maternity-care inequities
fellows.bio: Hall's research centers on the inequities experienced by rural maternity patients and how non-biological influences shape their access to care, experiences with providers, and health outcomes.

fellows.name: Joann Jabari
fellows.institution: Goldfarb School of Nursing, Barnes-Jewish College
fellows.focus: Loneliness and health among vulnerable populations
fellows.bio: Jabari focuses on the dynamic relationships between loneliness and health outcomes among vulnerable populations, designing interventions that mitigate non-biological factors that negatively influence health.

fellows.name: Rui Luo
fellows.focus: STI prevention among MSM using PrEP
fellows.bio: Luo examines how to prevent the rising trend of sexually transmitted infections among men who have sex with men who use pre-exposure prophylaxis, through behaviorally informed interventions.

fellows.name: Ibrahim Wunpini Mashoud
fellows.focus: Household solid fuels and cardiometabolic risk in Kenya
fellows.bio: Mashoud uses data from funded work in Kenya to assess the association between household solid fuels — firewood, kerosene, animal dung, and agricultural residue — and cardiometabolic disease outcomes in a low-income community.

fellows.name: Abdul-Manaf Mutaru
fellows.focus: Birth mode, breastfeeding and child well-being
fellows.bio: Mutaru investigates how mode of birth, breastfeeding, and maternal psychosocial factors influence child well-being using harmonized data from the Environmental Influences on Child Health Outcomes (ECHO) program.

fellows.name: Eric Peprah Osei
fellows.focus: Digital innovation and diabetes disparities
fellows.bio: Osei aims to integrate digital innovation with social and behavioral science to reduce disparities in diabetes outcomes, targeting structural barriers such as limited digital literacy and socioeconomic constraints.

fellows.name: Olivia Short
fellows.focus: Stigma, pain and violence
fellows.bio: Short researches stigma related to pain and violence, working to help providers conceptualize violence as a social driver of chronic pain and to create destigmatization interventions that improve pain care.

fellows.name: Edith Nnenna Utaka
fellows.focus: HPV self-sampling for cervical-cancer screening
fellows.bio: Utaka is conceptualizing a study exploring contextual factors that influence U.S. women's willingness to adopt HPV self-sample collection for cervical cancer screening, to inform strategies that reduce screening disparities.

fellows.name: Luke Velickoff
fellows.focus: Gender-affirming hormone therapy and renal function
fellows.bio: Velickoff studies the impact of estrogen-based gender-affirming hormone therapy on renal function and more accurate ways to assess renal health without sex-based coefficients in eGFR equations.
fellows.mentorExternal: Dr. Tonia Poteat, Dr. Chip Bailey

fellows.name: Nayung Youn
fellows.focus: AI-enabled self-management in cancer
fellows.bio: Youn's goal is to integrate AI-enabled technologies into self-management interventions for people with cancer, designing tools that account for social and structural barriers to care.

fellows.name: Hao Zhang
fellows.focus: Aging and chronic HIV (HAND)
fellows.bio: Zhang leads a study using the All of Us Research Program to investigate the intersection of aging and chronic HIV infection, specifically HIV-associated neurocognitive disorders.
